sections.title: Fats
sections.narration: 6g of fat. omega 3 is doing a lot of the work here. for a legume, this is exactly the kind of profile you want.
sections.subtitleText: 6g of fat. omega 3 is doing a lot of the work here. for a legume, this is exactly the kind of profile you want.

sections.title: Carbs
sections.narration: 60.7g of carbs. fibre is doing a lot of the work here. for legumes, the carbs look much better when fibre and protein are both backing them up.
sections.subtitleText: 60.7g of carbs. fibre is doing a lot of the work here. for legumes, the carbs look much better when fibre and protein are both backing them up.

sections.title: Proteins
sections.narration: 19.3g of protein. bioavailability and amino acid quality are both strong. protein support is a big part of what makes legumes worth it.
sections.subtitleText: 19.3g of protein. bioavailability and amino acid quality are both strong. protein support is a big part of what makes legumes worth it.

sections.title: Vitamins
sections.narration: everything else here is pretty flat.
sections.subtitleText: everything else here is pretty flat.

sections.title: Minerals
sections.narration: iron at 34% daily value, magnesium at 29% daily value, zinc at 27% daily value stand out most. useful, but still not a major mineral edge.
sections.subtitleText: iron at 34% daily value, magnesium at 29% daily value, zinc at 27% daily value stand out most. useful, but still not a major mineral edge.

sections.title: Pros
sections.narration: on the plus side: strong all-round legume profile because fibre, minerals, and useful protein all show up well enough to make it a very solid category food. easy to use across lots of meals because works in bowls, curries, salads, hummus, and snacks. pretty filling and steady because useful as a practical staple rather than just a niche health food.

sections.subtitleText: on the plus side: strong all-round legume profile because fibre, minerals, and useful protein all show up well enough to make it a very solid category food. easy to use across lots of meals because works in bowls, curries, salads, hummus, and snacks. pretty filling and steady because useful as a practical staple rather than just a niche health food.

sections.title: Cons
sections.narration: now the downsides: sugar is a bit high for the category because not a disaster, but it is less clean than the best legumes on this axis. can be rough on some people digestively because legume tolerance varies a lot. contains some anti-nutrients because phytates may reduce mineral uptake somewhat.

closing.summary: Chickpeas has a genuinely strong overall case. the practical downsides are real, but they do not outweigh the upside. best when you want fibre, protein, and actual meal utility together.
closing.useCaseNote: Best when you want fibre, protein, and actual meal utility together.
closing.cta: Would you rank it the same, or nah?

explanation.summary: Fats are carrying this food most, while minerals are the biggest drag. It lands in S tier.
explanation.whyThisTier: This food lands in S tier with an overall score of 62 from a base nutrition score of 58. Context lifted it by 4 points.
